headline: Comprehensive Eye Checkup in Chennai
subheadline: A complete eye health evaluation -- not just a vision test -- from AIIMS-trained Dr. Rajeswari in KK Nagar, Chennai.
metaDescription: Comprehensive eye checkup in Chennai with Dr. Rajeswari -- vision testing, eye pressure check, retina screening, and dry eye evaluation. Book online.

whyPatientsChoose.title: More Than a Vision Test
whyPatientsChoose.description: A full evaluation of eye health -- pressure, retina, and surface checks -- not just a glasses prescription.

symptoms: It's been over a year since your last eye exam
Mild blurring you've gotten used to ignoring
Eye strain after screen time or reading
Family history of glaucoma, diabetes, or retinal disease

processSteps.title: Vision & Refraction
processSteps.description: Precise testing to identify any refractive error and current glasses needs.

processSteps.title: Eye Health Screening
processSteps.description: Eye pressure check, retina evaluation, and dry eye assessment as part of the same visit.

processSteps.title: Results, Explained Clearly
processSteps.description: We walk you through every finding and what -- if anything -- needs follow-up.

landingFaqs.question: I don't have any symptoms -- do I really need a checkup?
landingFaqs.answer: Many eye conditions, including glaucoma and early diabetic retinopathy, have no noticeable symptoms until vision is already affected. A comprehensive checkup catches these early, when they're most treatable.

landingFaqs.question: How long does a comprehensive eye checkup take?
landingFaqs.answer: Typically 30-45 minutes, depending on whether pupil dilation is needed for a detailed retina examination.

landingFaqs.question: Will I need to get my eyes dilated?
landingFaqs.answer: Not always -- it depends on what we find during the initial examination and your individual risk factors. We'll explain if and why it's recommended for you.

educationalInsight.title: Why a Checkup Matters Even If You Feel Fine
educationalInsight.body: Vision changes gradually enough that most people adapt without realizing it -- and some of the most serious eye conditions, like glaucoma, cause no symptoms at all until permanent vision loss has already occurred. A comprehensive checkup looks beyond just whether you can read the chart clearly.

steps: Discussion of findings and any recommended follow-up

branches.outcome: We confirm your eye health is on track and recommend a routine follow-up interval.
We explain what we found, why it matters, and the most appropriate next step -- without overstating urgency.

prioritizeIf: It has been more than a year since your last eye exam
You have diabetes or a family history of glaucoma
You spend long hours on screens
You're over 40 and have never had a baseline eye pressure check

doctorsPerspectiveStructured.patientConcern: Patients often assume that if they can see clearly, their eyes must be healthy -- so a checkup feels unnecessary.
doctorsPerspectiveStructured.response: Clear vision and healthy eyes aren't always the same thing. Conditions like glaucoma can progress silently for years. A comprehensive checkup isn't about finding a problem -- it's about confirming there isn't one, or catching it early enough that it's easy to manage.

learnMoreLink.text: Read our full guide to comprehensive eye checkups
